title: centaurea pomeliana
intro: Centaurea pomeliana, also known as Mountain Cornflower, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and sprawling habit. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its chemical constituents such as alkaloids and glycosides. Centaurea pomeliana is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for use in herbal medicine, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
